Assembly: The assembly csSphPalu1.1 is based on 30x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The genome is tetraploid so this assembly represents one copy of each chromosome from each sub genome. This includes a single copy of a putative sex chromosome and multiple copies of a michrochromosome which appears to be underrepresented based on read coverage.

Assembly: The assembly xbAzoCham1.1 is based on 35x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. some telomeric repeat couldn't be uniquely assigned to a chromosomal location.

Assembly: The assembly iuAmpCont1.hap1.1 is based on 21x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. X chromosome assigned based on alignment to Psococerastis gibbosa (GCA_963971405.1) and Loensia variegata (GCA_964261705.1). Order and orientation of scaffolds on Chromosome 2 from ~55.80-59.21Mb is unsure.

Assembly: The assembly ilMacStel1.hap1.1 is based on 40x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The sex chromosomes were assigned based on the copy number on the diploid HiC map. The order and orientation on Chromosome W from 4.55 to 13.27 and from 14.19 to 17.11 Mbp may be uncertain.

Assembly: This project provides the genome assembly csSphBalt1.1 of the haploid Baltic bogmoss, Sphagnum balticum. The sample was provided by Olena Meleshko & Kristian Hassel, and the data generation and assembly were carried out by EBP-Nor (https://www.ebpnor.org/). csSphBalt1.1 is based on 60x PacBio HiFi data and 100x Arima Hi-C data. The HiFi reads were filtered for adapters using HiFiAdapterFilt, and assembled with hifiasm, generating a primary assembly. The assembly was purged for haplotigs with purge_dups, before scaffolding with YaHS. FCS-adaptor was used to remove putative adapters, and putative contaminants were removed with FCS-GX. The chromosomes confirmed by Hi-C data are named according to size.

Assembly: The assembly iyMegLeac1.1 is based on 62x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi and OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.
